name: stripe-create-refund
description: Issue a full or partial refund for a Stripe charge or payment intent. Use in customer support workflows, fraud handling, or automated refund policies. Supports {{key}} template interpolation for CHARGE_ID and PAYMENT_INTENT_ID so IDs from webhook events can be passed directly.

API_KEY_SECRET.description: Vault path to the Stripe secret key.

CHARGE_ID.description: Stripe charge ID (ch_xxx) to refund. Provide either CHARGE_ID or PAYMENT_INTENT_ID, not both. Supports {{charge_id}} interpolation.

PAYMENT_INTENT_ID.description: Stripe payment intent ID (pi_xxx) to refund. Provide either CHARGE_ID or PAYMENT_INTENT_ID, not both. Supports {{payment_intent_id}} interpolation.

AMOUNT.description: Amount to refund in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents for USD). If omitted, issues a full refund.

REASON.description: Refund reason: 'duplicate', 'fraudulent', or 'requested_by_customer'. Defaults to no reason if omitted.

METADATA.description: JSON object of key-value metadata to attach to the refund (e.g. '{"ticket_id": "cs_123"}').

setup_instructions.title: Charge vs Payment Intent
setup_instructions.detail: If your payment flow used PaymentIntents (recommended), use PAYMENT_INTENT_ID. If you used Charges directly, use CHARGE_ID. Only provide one.

setup_instructions.title: Partial refund
setup_instructions.detail: Set AMOUNT to refund only a portion (in cents). For example, AMOUNT=500 refunds $5.00 USD. Omit AMOUNT for a full refund.

setup_instructions.title: Typical support workflow
setup_instructions.detail: Chain: [support ticket webhook] → [stripe-create-refund: PAYMENT_INTENT_ID={{payment_intent_id}}, REASON=requested_by_customer] → [send refund confirmation email].
